clinical_history: This ECG, from a 57-year-old woman with dyspnea, is most consistent with which ONE of the following diagnoses?
diagnosis: The ECG shows resting sinus tachycardia (about 100 beats/min) with a single premature ventricular complex (PVC). Note the compensatory pause associated with the PVC. Peaked P waves, consistent with right atrial overload (P pulmonale pattern; P amplitude >2.5 mm tall in the inferior leads, with normal duration and with an inferior P wave axis) are present, along with relatively low limb lead voltage (also V1-V4) and slow R wave progression. This constellation of findings is consistent with an exacerbation of chronic obstructive lung disease (COPD). Slight non-specific ST-T changes are present.
